steps.title: Capture the dual record
steps.content: The passage focuses on Clara Ditzler Alspaugh's unusually detailed fabric scrapbook. It says she recorded personal memories, prices, and uses for fabric, and that the scrapbook represents fifty years of changing textures, patterns, and dress styles. The main idea must include both personal and historical value.

steps.title: Choose the main idea
steps.content: Choice A invents influence on other women. Choice B overgeneralizes to fabric scrapbooks broadly and omits Alspaugh as the focus. Choice C gives only the background point that many women kept fabric scrapbooks. Choice D centers Alspaugh's scrapbook and captures its two roles as an account of her life and a record of fashion trends. The answer is D.
